timeline.label: NSP-Minnesota signed electric service agreement to power a new Google data center; customer pays all incremental costs under Minnesota large-load rules.
PSCo recognized $287M Marshall wildfire litigation settlement charge; ongoing earnings exclude this one-time item.
Published $60B base capital plan for 2026-2030 ($11.6B in 2025 actual); funding mix ~50% cash from operations, ~38% debt, ~12% equity.
FY2025 dividends declared $2.28 per share (+4%); ongoing diluted EPS $3.80 vs GAAP $3.42.
2026 ongoing earnings guidance set at $4.04-$4.16 per share on rate-base recovery and load growth.
Purchased expanded wildfire insurance coverage (~$40M annual premium NSP system; $300M Colorado layer per 10-K).
Issued $5.4B common stock in 2025 (per equity roll-forward) to fund capex and maintain capital structure.
FY2024 dividends declared $2.19 per share; ongoing diluted EPS $3.50.
Accelerated renewables and transmission build-out across Minnesota, Colorado, Texas/New Mexico, and Wisconsin subsidiaries.
Marshall Fire ignited in Boulder County, Colorado (PSCo territory); litigation and settlement process began.

industry.structure: Xcel serves ~3.9 million electric and gas customers across eight states through four regulated utility subsidiaries (NSP-Minnesota, NSP-Wisconsin, PSCo, SPS). Returns are set by state and federal regulators on invested rate base, not market pricing.
industry.share_shift: Load growth is shifting toward large-load customers (AI data centers, industrial electrification) in Minnesota and Colorado; Xcel is winning interconnection agreements but must recover capital through rate cases and riders.
industry.trend: Utilities face simultaneous capex super-cycles (grid, renewables, transmission) and political affordability pressure; wildfire liability in Colorado and equity issuance to fund $60B of 2026-2030 capex are live capital-allocation themes.
